item.title: Rubien 0.1.0
item.description: Rubien 0.1.0 (Alpha). First public release. See GitHub release notes for details.

item.title: Rubien 0.1.1
item.description: Rubien 0.1.1 (Alpha). See GitHub release notes for details.

item.title: Rubien 0.1.2
item.description: Reader: the Clipped and Live tabs are now a single Clip tab with a Refresh button, plus a new Original tab that shows the live web page. Notion pages now clip with code blocks, math, images, toggles, and callouts preserved. Math in clipped articles keeps its color. Annotation popovers no longer clip at the right edge of the window.

item.title: Rubien 0.1.3
item.description: iCloud Sync fix: release builds now sync your library and PDFs to the correct iCloud (Production) environment, so all your Macs stay in sync — a fresh install previously came up empty. Plus a refreshed app icon.

item.title: Rubien 0.1.4
item.description: iCloud Sync fix: a fresh Mac now pulls your full library and PDFs instead of stalling partway through the first sync.

item.title: Rubien 0.1.5
item.description: iCloud Sync fix: custom properties (e.g. Method, Modality)

item.title: Rubien 0.1.6
item.description: • iCloud sync now pulls remote changes automatically — on launch, on focus, and periodically while open. • Fixed a sync stall where a cross-device tag conflict could halt all syncing; conflicts now self-resolve. • Deleting an in-use tag or select option now asks first; unused ones delete in one click. • Fixed an option-picker height glitch after canceling a delete.

item.title: Rubien 0.1.7
item.description: • Reader: refreshed liquid-glass annotation popovers and a cleaner, transparent note editor • Detail view: edit title, authors, abstract, and notes inline • MCP server now on npm: connect Claude to your library with npx -y rubien-mcp-server • Prebuilt Linux rubien-cli now ships with releases (signed, with in-place self-update)

item.title: Rubien 0.1.8
item.description: • Appearance — choose Light, Dark, or System in Settings → General. Applies across the whole app, including reader windows. • View icons — pick a custom icon for each view when creating or editing it. • BibTeX import — {capitalization-protection} braces are now stripped from titles and fields.

item.title: Rubien 0.1.10
item.description: • Fix a crash that could occur during iCloud sync • Search now ranks results by relevance (best match first), not just by date added • Re-adding a paper already in your library now confirms it and jumps to it, instead of silently doing nothing

item.title: Rubien 0.1.11
item.description: • New flat, left-aligned toolbar (Properties, Search, Add paper, Add website, Add manually, Import PDF). • Reference details now open in a floating, resizable glass panel — toggle it with the Details button. • The "Update ready" indicator now appears automatically and is easier to spot. • Web clips: more reliable extraction on single-page-app sites, plus correct in-page (#fragment) link handling.

item.title: Rubien 0.1.12
item.description:
UI polish: subtle hover highlights on table rows, the view chrome bar (Add filter / Sort / Group / Wrap), and the Properties menu make controls read as interactive. "Add manually" now lives in the import menu, and the stray keyboard focus ring that auto-highlighted the first control when a popover or sheet opened is gone.
Reliability: a database busy-timeout keeps concurrent writes from being silently dropped under load.

item.title: Rubien 0.1.13
item.description: Hover highlights now work reliably in every popover — the Properties panel, the tag and status pickers, the sort, group, and display menus, filters, and the date and color pickers. In the Tags picker, the delete icon now appears only on hover, matching the other pickers. And table rows no longer highlight when the pointer moves over the details panel.

item.title: Rubien 0.2.0
item.description:
Rubien 0.2.0 — the Assistant
• NEW: the Assistant — chat with Claude about the document you're reading, in both the PDF and web readers. A floating, resizable card that reads the paper through Rubien's own tools (text, figures, your highlights) and can search your library. Requires the Claude Code CLI (claude.ai/code) installed and signed in; configure under Settings ▸ Assistant. • Select text → Ask: quote a passage straight into the chat, with page numbers from PDFs. • History: browse, search the content of, and resume past conversations — transcripts restore in full. • Approvals: read-only tools run silently; anything that writes or runs shell commands asks first (or flip a conversation to Auto). • PDF reader: link previews and reveal controls; annotations moved into the left sidebar as a Notes tab, and the page reflows beside the assistant instead of being covered. • Note: Rubien is no longer sandboxed (required to run the assistant CLI). Your library, annotations, and iCloud sync are unaffected.

item.title: Rubien 0.2.1
item.description:
Codex support — switch between Claude Code and Codex per conversation (composer picker, or set a default in Settings → Assistant); model and reasoning effort are selectable.
Also in 0.2.1: • Reader — smoother sidebar hover and resize. • Assistant chat UX — the transcript no longer yanks your scroll while a reply streams (a "new messages" pill appears instead), plus unified chat iconography and quieter tool-call traces.

item.title: Rubien 0.2.2
item.description: • Scoped conversation history — the assistant's History opens on "This document", showing only conversations about the paper you're in; switch to "All documents" for everything. • The last backend choice (Claude ↔ Codex) is remembered for new conversations. • Improved assistant hints — the send shortcut (⌘+↩) is shown in the input box, and the start page is clearer. • Minor cleanup.

item.title: Rubien 0.2.3
item.description: • Assistant: chat is ready the moment a reader opens — no more “Checking assistant setup…” wait • Assistant: signing out mid-session is now detected; setup guidance + Recheck are available even in an open conversation • Assistant: fixed a race where switching backends could show the wrong setup status • Assistant: Ask no longer flips your assistant-panel preference for future windows; Settings shows the CLI path when installed but signed out • Reader: windows remember the last size you used (wider default) • Reader: hiding the notes/assistant panels now lets you make the web reader window narrower; showing one grows it to fit

item.title: Rubien 0.3.0
item.description:
Attach files to the assistant — add images and Markdown/text files to a chat message (file picker, drag-and-drop, or paste) in both readers. Images reach Claude and Codex natively, attachments survive History resume, and staged files stay in your Assistant workspace — nothing enters your library or sync.
Also in 0.3.0: • Import overhaul — a staged import flow with review-before-commit for PDFs, Markdown files, Zotero folders, and DOI/arXiv identifiers; imported Markdown reads like web content in the reader. • Agent full-text search — a new grep tool (rubien_grep_text; CLI: rubien-cli grep) searches inside PDF and web body text, page-anchored on PDFs and offset-anchored on web content. • Unified read tools (breaking) — rubien_read_text and rubien_read_annotations replace the four kind-specific PDF/web tools (CLI: read text / read annotations). rubien-mcp-server 0.1.x does not work with this build — update to rubien-mcp-server@0.2.0. • Codex model auto-discovery — the assistant model picker lists what your installed codex actually offers, with per-model reasoning efforts.

item.title: Rubien 0.3.1
item.description: • Mention papers from your library in Assistant conversations. • Paste or drag images and files directly into the Assistant composer. • Improved composer guidance and paper mention popover layout.

item.title: Rubien 0.3.2
item.description: • Add papers from eLife — paste an eLife article URL (elifesciences.org/articles/…) and Rubien resolves its metadata and downloads the open-access PDF. • Smaller download — a leaner, Apple Silicon-only app image.

item.title: Rubien 0.3.3
item.description: • Add papers from eNeuro URLs — Rubien resolves their metadata and downloads the open-access PDF. • PDF attachments no longer hang; attaching or replacing a PDF is now safe during concurrent operations, with clearer hover feedback. • Citation previews now match the reader theme. • Assistant composer guidance is more compact, and the chat sidebar can be resized wider.
